graph.label: TDT4300 Data Warehouse and Data Mining knowledge graph
design_notes: The graph follows the same item/edge/domain/view schema as the provided functional_analysis.json template.
Definitions, algorithms, processes, schemas, measures, exam recipes, and query patterns are first-class nodes.
Definitional dependencies differ from logical/algorithmic dependencies and pedagogical learning dependencies.
Exam recipes are explicit application nodes connected to the concepts they require.

source_refs.exam_set: TDT4300 exams 2021-2025 spring/resit, questions and selected solutions
primary_books: Han, Kamber, Pei: Data Mining: Concepts and Techniques, 3rd ed.
Tan, Steinbach, Kumar: Introduction to Data Mining

items.label: Data Warehouse and Data Mining
items.definition: Course covering data warehousing, OLAP, preprocessing, association rules, clustering, classification, and web usage mining.
items.intuition: A practical exam course about modelling analytical data and applying core data mining algorithms.

items.label: Data mining
items.definition: Extraction of useful, valid, and understandable patterns or models from data.
items.intuition: Turn stored data into patterns that can support decisions.

items.label: Knowledge Discovery Process (KDD)
items.statement: KDD consists of preprocessing, data mining, and post-processing.
items.definition: The end-to-end workflow for converting raw data into useful knowledge.
items.intuition: Most work happens before the algorithm: clean, integrate, transform, mine, evaluate.

items.label: Data preprocessing
items.definition: Cleaning, integration, transformation, reduction, discretization, and sampling applied before mining.
items.intuition: Make data valid and useful before trusting any model.

items.label: Data quality
items.definition: Degree to which data is accurate, complete, consistent, relevant, and non-redundant.
items.intuition: Garbage in, garbage out.

items.label: Missing values
items.definition: Absent attribute values requiring deletion, imputation, or model-aware handling.
items.intuition: Missingness can be noise or information; do not blindly fill it.

items.label: Noise and outliers
items.definition: Noise is random error; outliers are observations far from the main pattern and may be valid.
items.intuition: Outliers are not automatically mistakes.

items.label: Normalization / scaling
items.definition: Transform numeric attributes to comparable ranges or distributions.
items.intuition: Prevents large-scale attributes from dominating distance-based algorithms.

items.label: Discretization
items.definition: Convert continuous attributes into intervals or categorical bins.
items.intuition: Useful for concept hierarchies and some classification/association tasks.

items.label: Sampling
items.definition: Selecting a subset of records, e.g. simple random, with/without replacement, or stratified sampling.
items.intuition: Use smaller data while preserving relevant population structure.

items.label: Stratified sampling
items.definition: Split data into strata/classes and sample from each stratum, often proportionally.
items.intuition: Preserves minority or class structure better than blind random sampling.

items.label: Attribute types
items.definition: Nominal, binary, ordinal, interval, and ratio attributes determine which computations are meaningful.
items.intuition: Do not average IDs or take distances between arbitrary names.

items.label: Distance measures
items.definition: Functions measuring dissimilarity between objects, including L1/Manhattan, L2/Euclidean, and L∞/Chebyshev.
items.intuition: Geometry chosen by the distance measure determines nearest neighbors and clusters.

items.label: Cosine similarity
items.definition: Similarity based on angle rather than magnitude.
items.intuition: Common for sparse text vectors; ignores document length scale.

items.label: Curse of dimensionality
items.statement: As dimensionality increases, data become sparse and distance contrasts can weaken.
items.definition: Collection of effects making high-dimensional analysis harder.
items.intuition: Everything gets far away; nearest neighbor may stop meaning much.

items.label: Data warehouse
items.definition: Subject-oriented, integrated, time-variant, nonvolatile data store for analytical queries.
items.intuition: Historical, cleaned, read-mostly data arranged for analysis rather than transactions.

items.label: OLTP
items.definition: Operational transaction processing systems optimized for frequent inserts, updates, deletes, and concurrency.
items.intuition: Runs the business now.

items.label: OLAP
items.definition: Online analytical processing over multidimensional data using aggregation, slicing, dicing, pivoting, drill-down, and roll-up.
items.intuition: Ask aggregated business questions quickly from many angles.

items.label: Data mart
items.definition: A subject- or department-specific subset of warehouse data.
items.intuition: Smaller analytical store for one group, e.g. sales or HR.

items.label: Enterprise Data Warehouse
items.definition: Warehouse spanning subjects across the entire organization.
items.intuition: Central analytical memory for the company.

items.label: ETL
items.definition: Extract, transform, and load data from source systems into a warehouse.
items.intuition: Move dirty operational data into clean analytical form.

items.label: Fact table
items.definition: Central warehouse table storing measurements/events and foreign keys to dimensions.
items.intuition: The big table of things that happened.

items.label: Dimension table
items.definition: Descriptive table giving analysis axes such as time, item, location, customer, product, or device.
items.intuition: The ways facts can be grouped, filtered, and explained.

items.label: Measure
items.definition: Numerical quantity stored or derived in the fact table, e.g. sales, count, duration, price, quantity.
items.intuition: What you aggregate.

items.label: Concept hierarchy
items.definition: Ordered granularity levels in a dimension, e.g. day→month→year or city→country→continent.
items.intuition: The path for roll-up and drill-down.

items.label: Star schema
items.definition: Schema with one central fact table connected directly to denormalized dimension tables.
items.intuition: Simple, fast joins; redundancy is accepted for analytics.

items.label: Snowflake schema
items.definition: Normalized variant of a star schema where dimension hierarchies are split into multiple tables.
items.intuition: Less redundancy, more joins.

items.label: Fact constellation
items.definition: Multiple fact tables sharing dimension tables.
items.intuition: Several business processes analyzed through common dimensions.

items.label: Data cube
items.definition: Multidimensional model organizing measures by dimensions and hierarchy levels.
items.intuition: A generalized pivot table with dimensions as analysis axes.

items.label: Cuboid
items.definition: A particular group-by level combination in a data cube lattice.
items.intuition: One aggregate table/view from the cube.

items.label: Base cuboid
items.definition: The cuboid at the lowest granularity level.
items.intuition: Most detailed cube level.

items.label: Apex cuboid
items.definition: The all-level cuboid aggregating over all dimensions.
items.intuition: One grand total cell.

items.label: Cube lattice
items.definition: The partial order of cuboids from detailed to aggregate levels.
items.intuition: All possible roll-up states of a cube.

items.label: SQL CUBE operator
items.formal_statement: For n dimensions without hierarchies, CUBE generates 2^n group-by combinations; with m values per dimension, cells scale as (m+1)^n.
items.definition: Operator computing aggregations over all subsets of dimensions.
items.intuition: All GROUP BY combinations at once.

items.label: OLAP operation
items.definition: Transformation over cube granularity or selection: roll-up, drill-down, slice, dice, pivot.
items.intuition: Move around the cube.

items.label: ROLL-UP
items.definition: Aggregate from lower to higher hierarchy level, e.g. city→country or day→year.
items.intuition: Zoom out.

items.label: DRILL-DOWN
items.definition: Move from higher to lower hierarchy level, e.g. year→month→day.
items.intuition: Zoom in.

items.label: SLICE
items.definition: Fix one dimension value, reducing cube dimensionality.
items.intuition: Take one layer of the cube.

items.label: DICE
items.definition: Filter on multiple dimension values or ranges to form a subcube.
items.intuition: Cut out the relevant block.

items.label: PIVOT
items.definition: Rotate/reorient dimensions in a cross-tab or cube view.
items.intuition: Same data, different axes.

items.label: MDX
items.definition: Multidimensional expression language for querying OLAP cubes.
items.intuition: SQL-ish language for cube axes, members, measures, and slicers.

items.label: Slicer axis
items.definition: MDX WHERE clause restricting the cube context without placing the dimension on rows or columns.
items.intuition: A global cube filter.

items.label: MOLAP
items.definition: OLAP architecture using array-based multidimensional storage engines.
items.intuition: Store cube-like arrays directly for fast lookup and aggregation.

items.label: ROLAP
items.definition: OLAP architecture storing warehouse data in relational tables and answering OLAP through SQL.
items.intuition: Cubes on top of relational databases.

items.label: HOLAP
items.definition: Hybrid OLAP combining relational storage for detail with multidimensional storage for aggregates.
items.intuition: Use relational scale and MOLAP speed where each fits.

items.label: Cube materialization
items.definition: Precomputing cuboids to speed up OLAP query processing.
items.intuition: Trade storage and refresh cost for low query latency.

items.label: Full materialization
items.definition: Precompute every cuboid in the cube lattice.
items.intuition: Fast queries, huge memory/storage.

items.label: Partial materialization
items.definition: Precompute a selected subset of useful cuboids.
items.intuition: Compromise between speed and storage.

items.label: Iceberg cube
items.definition: Cube containing only cells satisfying an aggregate threshold such as min support.
items.intuition: Store only large/interesting aggregates.

items.label: Bitmap index
items.definition: For each attribute value, store a bit vector marking rows/facts where that value occurs.
items.intuition: Boolean AND/OR bit operations answer low-cardinality filters fast.

items.label: Join index
items.definition: Precomputed mapping from dimension values/row IDs to fact table rows participating in joins.
items.intuition: Skip expensive star joins by remembering which fact rows match.

items.label: B-tree index
items.definition: Tree index suited for selective lookups and ranges, less ideal for low-cardinality warehouse attributes.
items.intuition: Great for OLTP-style point/range lookup; often not the best star-schema bitmap filter tool.

items.label: Star-join query
items.definition: Query joining a fact table to multiple dimensions, filtering on dimension attributes, then grouping measures.
items.intuition: Classic warehouse query targeted by bitmap/join indexes.

items.label: Association rule mining
items.definition: Find frequent itemsets and implication-like rules in transaction data.
items.intuition: Market basket analysis: items that occur together.

items.label: Transaction database
items.definition: Collection of transactions, each containing a set of items.
items.intuition: Rows are baskets/playlists/sessions; entries are items.

items.label: Itemset
items.definition: A set of items occurring in transactions.
items.intuition: Candidate pattern in baskets.

items.label: Support
items.definition: Fraction or count of transactions containing an itemset.
items.intuition: How common a pattern is.

items.label: Frequent itemset
items.definition: Itemset whose support is at least minsup.
items.intuition: A pattern common enough to be considered.

items.label: Confidence
items.definition: Conditional probability-like strength of a rule.
items.intuition: Among transactions with X, how often Y also appears.

items.label: Lift
items.definition: Association strength relative to independence.
items.intuition: Confidence corrected for how common Y already is.

items.label: Association rule
items.definition: Rule generated from a frequent itemset using confidence threshold.
items.intuition: When X appears, Y tends to appear.

items.label: Apriori algorithm
items.statement: Generate candidate k-itemsets from frequent (k-1)-itemsets and prune using downward closure.
items.definition: Level-wise frequent itemset mining algorithm using the Apriori property.
items.intuition: If a set is frequent, all subsets must be frequent; if a subset is infrequent, prune the superset.

items.label: Apriori property
items.statement: All nonempty subsets of a frequent itemset are frequent; any superset of an infrequent itemset is infrequent.
items.definition: Anti-monotonicity of support.
items.intuition: Pruning engine of Apriori.

items.label: FP-growth
items.definition: Frequent pattern-growth algorithm using a compressed FP-tree and conditional pattern bases instead of candidate generation.
items.intuition: Compress transactions once, then mine conditional subtrees.

items.label: FP-tree
items.definition: Compressed prefix tree storing frequent items in support-descending order with counts and links.
items.intuition: Shared transaction prefixes reduce repeated scanning.

items.label: Conditional pattern base
items.definition: Set of prefix paths co-occurring with a suffix item in an FP-tree.
items.intuition: The local transaction database for one suffix pattern.

items.label: ECLAT algorithm
items.definition: Frequent itemset mining using vertical TID-set intersections.
items.intuition: Support becomes set intersection size.

items.label: TID-set
items.definition: Set of transaction IDs containing an itemset.
items.intuition: Vertical representation of transaction data.

items.label: Clustering
items.definition: Unsupervised grouping of objects so that objects in the same cluster are more similar than those in different clusters.
items.intuition: Find natural groups without labels.

items.label: K-means clustering
items.definition: Partitioning algorithm alternating between assigning points to nearest centroid and updating centroids.
items.intuition: Fit k spherical-ish clusters by moving means.

items.label: Centroid
items.definition: Mean representative of a cluster.
items.intuition: Cluster center for numeric data.

items.label: Silhouette coefficient
items.definition: Cluster validation measure comparing within-cluster distance a(i) with nearest-other-cluster distance b(i).
items.intuition: Near 1 means well placed; near 0 boundary; negative likely wrong cluster.

items.label: Hierarchical clustering
items.definition: Build nested clusters using agglomerative merging or divisive splitting.
items.intuition: A dendrogram of groups at multiple scales.

items.label: Linkage
items.definition: Distance between clusters, e.g. single-link, complete-link, average-link.
items.intuition: How HAC decides which clusters are closest.

items.label: DBSCAN
items.definition: Density-based clustering using Eps and MinPts to form clusters of density-connected points.
items.intuition: Find dense blobs and mark sparse leftovers as noise.

items.label: Eps-neighborhood
items.definition: Set of points within distance Eps of a given point.
items.intuition: Local neighborhood for DBSCAN density.

items.label: Core, border, and noise points
items.definition: Core has at least MinPts points in Eps-neighborhood; border is reachable from a core but not core; noise is neither.
items.intuition: DBSCAN labels points by local density role.

items.label: Cluster validation
items.definition: Assess clustering quality using internal, external, or relative measures.
items.intuition: Unsupervised output still needs sanity checks.

items.label: Classification
items.definition: Supervised learning task of assigning records to predefined classes.
items.intuition: Learn labels from labelled examples.

items.label: Train/test split
items.definition: Partition labelled data into training data used for fitting and test data used for evaluation.
items.intuition: Do not evaluate on the same data you trained on.

items.label: k-Nearest Neighbours
items.definition: Classify by majority label among the k closest training examples.
items.intuition: Local voting in feature space.

items.label: Decision tree
items.definition: Tree classifier splitting records by attribute tests until leaves predict class labels.
items.intuition: Nested if-then rules learned from data.

items.label: Split criterion
items.definition: Impurity or gain measure used to choose decision-tree splits.
items.intuition: Pick the split that makes child nodes purer.

items.label: Gini index
items.definition: Impurity measure for class distribution in a node.
items.intuition: Zero means pure; higher means mixed labels.

items.label: Entropy and information gain
items.definition: Impurity and reduction in impurity used by decision-tree algorithms.
items.intuition: Prefer splits that reduce uncertainty most.

items.label: Tree pruning
items.definition: Reduce decision-tree complexity by removing branches to improve generalization.
items.intuition: Fight overfitting by cutting brittle details.

items.label: Stopping conditions
items.definition: Rules to stop splitting, e.g. pure node, no attributes, too few records, max depth, or no gain.
items.intuition: Prevent infinite or useless tree growth.

items.label: Confusion matrix
items.definition: Table of predicted vs actual labels: TP, FP, TN, FN.
items.intuition: Shows exactly what kinds of errors a classifier makes.

items.label: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1
items.definition: Standard classification evaluation metrics.
items.intuition: Accuracy counts total correctness; precision and recall separate false positives/false negatives.

items.label: Overfitting
items.definition: Model fits training data too closely and generalizes poorly.
items.intuition: Memorizing the exam solutions rather than learning the method.

items.label: Web mining
items.definition: Mining useful knowledge from web content, hyperlink structure, and usage logs.
items.intuition: Data mining applied to pages, links, and user behavior.

items.label: Web content mining
items.definition: Extract information from page content such as terms, text, metadata, and documents.
items.intuition: Mine what pages say.

items.label: Web usage mining
items.definition: Mine user behavior patterns from web/server logs and interactions.
items.intuition: Mine what users do.

items.label: Web structure mining
items.definition: Mine hyperlink graph structure between pages.
items.intuition: Mine how pages point to each other.

items.label: Web log preprocessing
items.definition: Clean logs, identify users/sessions/resources, remove irrelevant requests, and build analysis matrices.
items.intuition: Raw log lines must become user-resource events.

items.label: User-resource matrix
items.definition: Binary/count matrix associating users with accessed web resources.
items.intuition: Rows are users, columns are pages/resources.

items.label: Resource-term matrix
items.definition: Matrix associating resources with terms extracted from their content.
items.intuition: Rows are documents/resources, columns are words.

items.label: Schema design exam recipe
items.statement: Identify grain, facts/measures, dimensions, hierarchies, then draw star or snowflake schema.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for warehouse modelling tasks.
items.intuition: Start with what one fact row means; everything else follows.

items.label: OLAP sequence exam recipe
items.statement: Translate query text into roll-up/drill-down/slice/dice/pivot operations.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for OLAP operation questions.
items.intuition: Map every phrase: where? when? what category? which filter?

items.label: Indexing exam recipe
items.statement: For low-cardinality warehouse filters use bitmap indexes; for star joins use join indexes.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for bitmap/join index questions.
items.intuition: Turn filters into bit-vector intersections and joins into precomputed row mappings.

items.label: Apriori exam recipe
items.statement: Compute L1, generate C2/L2, continue by join+prune, then derive rules with confidence.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for Apriori itemset/rule questions.
items.intuition: Count, prune, join, count, then rules.

items.label: FP-growth exam recipe
items.statement: Count item supports, filter/order items, build FP-tree, mine conditional pattern bases and conditional FP-trees.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for FP-growth questions.
items.intuition: Compress first, mine recursively.

items.label: ECLAT exam recipe
items.statement: Convert items to TID-sets, intersect TID-sets, keep itemsets meeting min support.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for ECLAT questions.
items.intuition: Support by set intersection.

items.label: DBSCAN exam recipe
items.statement: Compute Eps-neighborhoods, mark core/border/noise, connect density-reachable cores into clusters.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for DBSCAN point-set questions.
items.intuition: Count neighbors, then grow dense regions.

items.label: Decision-tree exam recipe
items.statement: Compute class distribution, impurity before split, weighted child impurity, choose best reduction; mention pruning/stopping.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for decision-tree questions.
items.intuition: Pure children are good; overgrown trees overfit.

items.label: Clustering method choice recipe
items.statement: Choose K-means for known k/spherical numeric clusters, HAC for hierarchy/small data, DBSCAN for arbitrary shapes/noise.
items.definition: Reusable answer template for conceptual clustering-method selection.
items.intuition: Match algorithm assumptions to data geometry.

edges.label: star schema contrasts with snowflake schema
data cube can be computed using CUBE
bitmap indexes speed up filters in star-join queries
join indexes speed up star-join queries
K-means can be evaluated by silhouette coefficient
Decision tree can use Gini index
Decision tree can use information gain
web usage mining constructs user-resource matrices
web content mining constructs resource-term matrices

concept_map.description: Broad semantic map of TDT4300 concepts, algorithms, schemas, measures, and exam recipes.

warehouse_olap_view.description: Data warehouse modelling, OLAP operations, cube materialization, and indexing.

algorithm_view.description: Mining algorithms and their key data structures/measures.

exam_recipe_view.description: Reusable answer templates for high-frequency exam task types.

preprocessing_similarity_view.description: Data preprocessing, attribute semantics, distance/similarity, and dimensionality.

prerequisite_map.description: Pedagogical learning order extracted from dependencies.

query_model.description: Query by filtering items by kind/domain/tags/exam_priority and traversing edges by type and dependency_class. Definitional dependencies state what is needed to define an item; logical dependencies state algorithmic/result usage; construction dependencies state data-structure/schema construction; pedagogical dependencies state useful learning order.
query_model.node_lookup: Items are addressed by stable snake_case id. Edges are interpreted literally as source --type--> target.
query_model.multigraph_rule: Multiple edges between the same source and target may coexist when they express different dependency classes or relations.
query_model.recommended_exam_filter: metadata.exam_priority in {main_pattern, recurring} OR metadata.tags contains exam

example_queries.expected_output: Fact table, dimension tables, measures, concept hierarchies, star/snowflake schema.
Roll-up, drill-down, slice, dice, pivot, and concept hierarchy dependencies.
Apriori uses candidate generation and Apriori property; FP-growth uses FP-tree and conditional pattern bases; ECLAT uses TID-set intersections.
